title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast. Get the step-by-step guide!
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro, served with rice and naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our Classic Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani – a true Indian culinary delight.

description: Experience the ultimate comfort food with our Classic Butter Chicken, a rich and creamy Murgh Makhani that melts in your mouth. This beloved Indian dish features succulent chicken pieces simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, infused with aromatic spices and finished with a swirl of butter and cream. Perfect for a special occasion or a delightful family meal, this recipe brings the authentic taste of India straight to your kitchen.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces, yogurt, 1/2 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix everything thoroughly, ensuring the chicken is well coated. Cover the bowl and let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or for 2-4 hours in the refrigerator for best results.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook the chicken for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until it is browned on all sides and about 70-80% cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set it aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatic Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and 1 tablespoon of butter. Reduce the heat to medium. Add the finely chopped onion and cook, stirring frequently, until it turns translucent and lightly golden brown, which should take about 5-7 minutes. Next, add the remaining 1/2 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and the slit green chilies (if using). Sauté for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Pour in the pureed tomatoes into the pan. Add a pinch of salt and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder. Stir well to combine all ingredients. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes. Continue cooking until the oil begins to separate from the tomato mixture, indicating that the tomatoes are well cooked and the flavors have deepened.

instructions.title: Create a Smooth Makhani Sauce
instructions.text: Add the pre-soaked and drained cashews to the tomato mixture in the pan. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Carefully transfer the entire contents of the pan into a blender. Blend until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy. For an exceptionally smooth restaurant-style gravy, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pan, discarding any solids.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Return the smooth, blended gravy to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the previously cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir in the sugar and the crushed dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). Mix everything gently. Allow the chicken to simmer in the rich sauce for 10-15 minutes, ensuring it is fully cooked and has absorbed all the wonderful flavors of the Makhani gravy.

instructions.title: Finish with Cream and Butter
instructions.text: Finally, stir in the heavy cream and the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, heating gently without bringing the gravy to a boil after adding the cream to prevent curdling. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, adding more salt or sugar to balance the flavors. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For an extra tender chicken, marinate overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the flavors to penetrate deeply and tenderize the meat, making your Butter Chicken even more succulent.

To achieve a truly restaurant-style smooth gravy, blend the tomato and cashew mixture thoroughly, then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve before adding the chicken. This removes any small bits of onion or tomato skin.
Serve your Murgh Makhani with warm naan bread, roti, or fluffy basmati rice to soak up all the delicious, creamy gravy. A final sprinkle of fresh cilantro adds a burst of freshness and color.
article:
The aroma of simmering spices, the vibrant hue of a rich tomato gravy, and the promise of tender, succulent chicken – that’s the magic of Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian dish, beloved worldwide, holds a special place in the hearts of food enthusiasts. Its creamy, subtly sweet, and tangy profile makes it an irresistible comfort food, perfect for family dinners, celebratory meals, or simply a treat to yourself. If you’ve ever dreamt of recreating that authentic, restaurant-quality taste in your own kitchen, you’re in the right place. Get ready to embark on a culinary journey to master this timeless classic.

## What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, shortly after the partition. It was reputedly created by Kundan Lal Jaggi, who combined leftover tandoori chicken with a rich, tomato-based gravy enriched with butter and cream. This innovative dish quickly captured the hearts of many and evolved into the global phenomenon we know today.

The essence of Butter Chicken lies in its exquisite balance of flavors and textures. It typically features succulent pieces of chicken (often marinated in yogurt and spices and traditionally cooked in a tandoor oven, though home cooks can grill, pan-fry, or bake it) swimming in a velvety, slightly sweet, and tangy sauce. Key ingredients for the sauce include pureed tomatoes, ginger, garlic, a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala and fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), and generous amounts of butter and cream. The result is a dish that is deeply satisfying, comforting, and utterly delicious, making it one of the most celebrated Indian chicken recipes.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your own masterpiece of Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani, gather these fresh and aromatic ingredients:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

**For the Makhani Sauce:**
*   2 tablespoons ghee or unsalted butter
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1-2 green chilies, slit lengthwise (optional, adjust to taste)
*   2 cans (800g total) crushed tomatoes or tomato puree
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, then drained)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), lightly crushed
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste, balances the tanginess)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter, extra, for finishing
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve a truly authentic and delicious Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring every piece of chicken is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best results.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Preheat your grill, oven broiler, or a heavy-bottomed pan with a little oil over medium-high heat. Cook the marinated chicken pieces until they are lightly charred and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside. The charring adds a crucial smoky depth to the final dish.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the ghee or 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they are soft and translucent, about 8-10 minutes.
4.  **Aromatics and Tomatoes:** Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using) to the pot. Sauté for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, red chili powder, garam masala, kasuri methi, sugar, and salt. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld.
5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the cooked sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until absolutely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot. Discard any solids caught in the sieve.
6.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot over low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, heavy cream, and the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter. Simmer gently for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce and the sauce to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust seasoning (salt, sugar) if necessary.
7.  **Serve:** Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect restaurant-style Butter Chicken at home is all about paying attention to a few key details:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The yogurt tenderizes the chicken, and the spices infuse it with flavor, making a significant difference to the final taste and texture. Overnight marination is truly worth it.
*   **Char the Chicken:** Whether you grill, broil, or pan-fry, aim for some slight charring on the chicken pieces. This mimics the smoky flavor of a tandoor oven and adds incredible depth to the dish.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use high-quality canned crushed tomatoes or fresh, ripe, pureed tomatoes. The tomatoes form the backbone of the sauce, so their flavor is paramount.
*   **Blend Until Smooth:** A truly classic Murgh Makhani sauce is velvety smooth. Don't rush the blending process. If you don't have a powerful blender, consider straining the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve after blending to remove any fibrous bits.
*   **Kasuri Methi:** Toasting the kasuri methi lightly in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding it to the sauce enhances its aromatic qualities significantly.
*   **Balance is Everything:** Butter Chicken is known for its balance of sweet, tangy, and savory notes. Don't be afraid to adjust the sugar and salt to your personal preference at the end. A final dollop of butter and swirl of cream are essential for that signature richness.

## Serving Suggestions

Pairing your homemade Butter Chicken with the right accompaniments elevates the entire dining experience.
*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (especially garlic naan) is the ultimate partner for scooping up every last drop of that luscious sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera (cumin) rice provides a neutral base that perfectly complements the rich flavors of the curry.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber or mint raita (yogurt dip) helps to balance the richness and mild spice of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime or lemon wedge right before serving adds brightness and freshness.
*   **Onions:** Sliced red onions, sometimes marinated in a little vinegar, offer a crisp, tangy counterpoint.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, you can certainly adapt it to suit different tastes or dietary needs:
*   **Vegetarian Version:** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas for a delicious Paneer Makhani or Vegetable Makhani.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder and green chilies to make it milder or spicier. For a beautiful red color without too much heat, use Kashmiri red chili powder.
*   **Creaminess:** For a dairy-free option, use full-fat coconut milk instead of heavy cream. For extra richness and thickness, increase the quantity of cashews in the sauce. Some recipes also use almond paste.
*   **Sweetness:** A touch of honey or maple syrup can replace sugar for a slightly different sweetness profile.
*   **Smokiness:** If you want to achieve an even deeper smoky flavor without a tandoor, try the "dhungar" method: place a small heat-proof bowl with a burning piece of charcoal in the pot, drizzle a little ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes.

Mastering this classic Butter Chicken recipe is a rewarding experience that brings the flavors of India right into your home. With its tender chicken, creamy tomato sauce, and perfectly balanced spices, it's a dish that consistently delights. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to impress your taste buds and your loved ones with your very own, incredibly delicious Murgh Makhani. Enjoy the journey of creating this timeless Indian culinary gem!
